S65 — Hotel catalogue import via the unified Import/Export UI

End-to-end walkthrough: uploading a booking catalogue (categories + resources) through the admin UI, with a screenshot at every step.

Generated 2026-06-11 · target: local stack (fe-admin :8081, backend :5000)

5
Categories imported
15
Resources imported
0
Errors
12
Documented steps

What this proves

The S61–S64 unified import/export exchangers are live in the data-exchange manifest, and a complete hotel booking catalogue can be seeded through the admin UI alone — no SQL, no bespoke importer. Categories and resources are linked by slug (category_slugs), resolved on import; the importer is dry-run-able and idempotent (re-import upserts by slug, never duplicates).

Import order matters. Resources reference their hotel category by slug, so the booking_categories envelope is imported (committed) before booking_resources. Importing resources first yields per-row unknown category_slug errors — by design (skip-with-error, never crash).

The envelopes (committed under docs/marketing/cms-imports/hotel/booking/)

booking_categories.json — 5 hotels

  • grand-marina — Grand Marina Hotel
  • alpine-lodge — Alpine Lodge Resort
  • seaside-resort — Seaside Resort & Spa
  • city-center — City Center Business Hotel
  • lakeview-retreat — Lakeview Retreat

booking_resources.json — 15 resources

Walkthrough — step by step

1

Admin login

The vbwd admin backoffice login page (http://localhost:8081/admin/login).

2

Signed in — dashboard

Authenticated as admin@example.com. The admin dashboard loads with the full backoffice sidebar (Sales, Settings, CMS).

3

Import / Export view

Settings → Import/Export. The General tab is manifest-driven: every registered entity (CMS, Sales, Settings clusters) can be exported or imported here. We will import the hotel booking catalogue — first categories, then resources.

4

Booking Categories: file selected

The envelope booking_categories.json is selected in the unified Import block. Mode is upsert (match existing rows by slug). No data has been written yet.

5

Booking Categories: dry-run preview

Clicking Preview runs a dry_run import — it validates and reports what would happen without writing. Result for booking_categories: created 5, updated 0, skipped 0, errors 0.

entity: booking_categoriescreated 5updated 0skipped 0errors 0
6

Booking Categories: import committed

Clicking Confirm commits the import. Result for booking_categories: created 5, updated 0, skipped 0, errors 0. The rows are now persisted in the database.

entity: booking_categoriescreated 5updated 0skipped 0errors 0
7

Booking Resources: file selected

The envelope booking_resources.json is selected in the unified Import block. Mode is upsert (match existing rows by slug). No data has been written yet.

8

Booking Resources: dry-run preview

Clicking Preview runs a dry_run import — it validates and reports what would happen without writing. Result for booking_resources: created 15, updated 0, skipped 0, errors 0.

entity: booking_resourcescreated 15updated 0skipped 0errors 0
9

Booking Resources: import committed

Clicking Confirm commits the import. Result for booking_resources: created 15, updated 0, skipped 0, errors 0. The rows are now persisted in the database.

entity: booking_resourcescreated 15updated 0skipped 0errors 0
10

Idempotent re-import (upsert by slug)

Re-importing the same resources envelope in upsert mode matches the existing rows by slug: created 0, updated 15, errors 0. No duplicate rows are created — the import is idempotent and safe to re-run.

entity: booking_resourcescreated 0updated 15skipped 0errors 0
11

Verify: Booking dashboard counts

The Booking admin dashboard reflects the import: 24 Resources (9 original + 15 imported) and 9 Categories (4 original + 5 hotel categories). The catalogue grew by exactly what we uploaded.

12

Verify: admin Resource List

The Booking → Resources list now shows the imported hotel resources (Superior Deluxe, Deluxe Room Sea View, SPA Full Day, Conference Room A, Family Room, Meditation Session, …), each linked to its hotel category via category_slugs. The catalogue imported through the UI is fully operational — bookable, with availability and pricing intact.

Result

✅ Categories created: 5 · Resources created: 15 · Errors: 0 · Re-import: updated 15, created 0 (idempotent). The imported resources appear in the admin Resource List with their hotel category, price, and capacity, and the booking dashboard counts grew by exactly the uploaded amount.

Out of scope for this import. The public storefront page at /booking is a CMS page (slug booking) and is not configured on this dev instance, so the public catalogue render is verified via the admin Resource List + the public /api/v1/booking/resources endpoint (24 resources, 15 of them the imported hotel rows) rather than the public page. Checkout proof (Stripe test card + tokens, with and without a coupon) is the remaining S65 phase.
